source_scope: Complete docs/product-spec.md retained as 10_一手正文/001_正文.md.

source_anchors.what_text_says: ReaderLab converts complex materials into Chinese-readable companion packages for reading, annotation, discussion, and capture.
supports: ReaderLab is a material absorption system, not only a formatter.
does_not_support: It does not prove any external book package is complete.

source_anchors.what_text_says: ReaderLab is not a summarizer, translator, full-book generator, automatic knowledge-base promoter, or UI/database project.
supports: The product is bounded by explicit non-goals.
does_not_support: It does not support replacing the reading workflow with AI conclusions.

source_anchors.what_text_says: First-hand materials come first and AI companionship cannot stand in front of the body.
supports: The body track must be primary.
does_not_support: It does not support using a fluent explanation as the source body.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Output starts from material type and assigns body, AI companion, and audit to separate places.
does_not_support: It does not support one universal summary format.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Books and longform materials keep original body and order by default.
supports: Longform body text must not be compressed or rewritten by default.
does_not_support: It does not prove the source is an external book.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Source refs, location map, claim trace, statuses, and decisions go into audit rather than reader-facing text.
supports: Audit and reader-facing layers should be separated.
does_not_support: It does not make audit fields a reader-facing structure.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Different statuses distinguish explanation quality, reading package completion, method probes, and Skill drafting.
supports: The product needs precise completion language.
does_not_support: It does not allow local explanation success to become package success.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Candidate capture is output only; promotion is not automatic.
supports: Human confirmation remains part of durable capture.
does_not_support: It does not support automatic promotion into LifeAtlas formal areas.

baseline_summary_trap: A shallow summary would say ReaderLab converts books and documents into Markdown packages with body text, AI explanation, audit files, and future Skill potential.
upgrade_question: Under what constraints can a reading product use AI to reduce the cost of understanding complex material while preserving the reader's direct contact with the source and avoiding false completion claims?

input: Complex material such as books, longform documents, courses, code docs, or Skill packages.
A reader who needs Chinese-readable material, annotations, discussion, and possible durable capture.
constraint: AI must not replace first-hand source reading.
Material types require different body rules.
Audit evidence must not pollute reader-facing prose.
Human acceptance remains separate from machine validation.
action: Preserve or clean the body according to material type.
Write AI companionship after or beside the body.
Record source scope, claim tiers, candidate decisions, and risks in audit.
Leave promotion to later human confirmation.
feedback: Reader annotations near the body reveal what still needs explanation.
Eval gates catch summary substitution, overreach, and premature promotion.
output: A companion package that keeps body, explanation, audit, and capture candidates separated.
A reader-facing page that helps understanding without becoming the source.
failure_mode: A local explanation success is mislabeled as complete package success.
Good-sounding insight is turned into a Skill too early.
boundary: This demo uses repo-owned longform material and does not validate external books.
Writer output still requires independent reader evaluation.

lens_candidates.text_trigger: The spec separates body, AI companion, audit, and human confirmation.
lens_candidates.clarifies: Shows why each layer has a different authority and why reader-facing prose should not carry audit machinery.
lens_candidates.boundary_power: Prevents interpreting the package as a single generated document.
lens_candidates.reader_gain: Reader sees the product as a controlled allocation of trust rather than a content generator.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: May over-formalize a product spec if used as a bureaucratic frame.

lens_candidates.text_trigger: Books and longform materials keep original body by default; engineering materials use cleaned body.
lens_candidates.clarifies: Explains why ReaderLab changes its operation based on material type instead of applying universal summarization.
lens_candidates.boundary_power: Keeps the demo from claiming external book validation.
lens_candidates.reader_gain: Reader understands the body rules as a product safety mechanism.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: Could become a slogan unless tied to actual artifact layout.

lens_candidates.text_trigger: Candidate capture is output only and does not auto-promote.
lens_candidates.clarifies: Shows how ReaderLab prevents temporary reading insights from becoming durable knowledge too quickly.
lens_candidates.boundary_power: Useful for explaining capture, but less central to Demo A's body-track proof.
lens_candidates.reader_gain: Reader understands why candidate sedimentation remains guarded.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: Can distract from the first-hand body requirement.

lens_candidates.text_trigger: The selected source is repo-owned product spec.
lens_candidates.clarifies: It does not clarify the demo; it overstates what this source can prove.
lens_candidates.boundary_power: Rejecting this lens protects the source scope.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: High risk of claiming too much from a repo-owned document.

absorb: Use first-hand body preservation as the non-negotiable starting point for longform packages.
Keep reader-facing prose natural and separate from audit evidence.
downgrade: Treat preserve-accompany-capture as a helpful reading frame, not yet a proven reusable Skill.
Treat knowledge capture as a later human-confirmed step, not a demo success criterion.
reject: Do not claim this repo-owned source validates external books.
Do not turn this demo into a formal ReaderLab Skill draft.

reader_facing_explanation.title: 读这份规格时，先看它怎样保护“正文”
reader_facing_explanation.body: See 20_AI陪读/001_reader-facing.md for the reader-facing prose. It uses only natural companion language and points back to 10_一手正文/001_正文.md.

delta_eval.reader_gain: The explanation reframes the product spec as a trust and authority design problem: how AI can assist reading without replacing the source or overstating completion.
